scope_note: QA Harmonic Chemistry LCM cert [323]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1995) Quantum Arithmetic Book 3 (QA-3), ITAM Portland, ISBN 1-883401-08-9, Chapter 5: 'CHEMISTRY'. Applies harmonic aliquot theory (cert [322]) to wave chemistry in the Myriad of Light/Chemism interface. Five structural results certified: (C1) LCM harmonic cycle identity: for all 20 Cosmos harmonic dyads, lcm(d1,d2) = d1*p2 = d2*p1 = A*p1*p2 exactly — this is the QA algebraic expression of 'Wave 1 x unique(2) = Wave 2 x unique(1) = Harmonic Cycle' (Iverson p.62). (C2) 3-wave composite LCM: for all C(6,3)=20 3-subsets of prime Cosmos d-values {3,5,7,11,13,17}, lcm(d1,d2,d3) = d1*d2*d3 (since pairwise coprime). (C3) C(n,2) pairing law: n mutually harmonic wavelets form exactly C(n,2)=n*(n-1)/2 harmonic pairs; for n=7: C(7,2)=21 (Iverson: 'They will make 21 different pairs', p.68); mod-9 Cosmos maximum mutual-harmonic group has size 6 (the 6 prime d-values), so n=7 lies beyond the mod-9 state space. (C4) L = b*e*d*a wavelength product (Iverson p.72: 'wavelength relates directly to the product of all four integers'): all 72 Cosmos L values positive, min=24 at (2,1), max=31824 at (8,9). (C5) Universal 2*3 bond: b*e*d*a divisible by 6 for ALL 72 Cosmos pairs — QA expression of 'all Quantum Waves must be some multiple of 6' (Iverson p.67). Builds on certs [322] (harmonic aliquot), [318] (synchronous harmonics ceiling). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
